message:
fix(tests): clear tokio runtime handle from TLS after test teardown across 43 modules

Add clear_runtime_handle() calls after dropping the tokio runtime in test
harnesses to prevent thread-local storage (TLS) panics during thread exit.

When a tokio runtime is dropped, its Handle may still be stored in thread-local
storage. If the TLS destructor runs after the runtime is gone, it triggers a
panic-during-drop that manifests as intermittent test failures with
"cannot drop a runtime in a context where blocking is not allowed" or similar
thread-local teardown errors.

The fix follows the same pattern in all affected modules: after the guarded
runtime drop, catch_unwind a call to crate::runtime_compat::clear_runtime_handle()
to ensure the TLS slot is clean regardless of whether the drop itself panicked.

Affected modules: approval, cleanup, diagnostic, distributed, events, export,
metrics, native_events, notifications, orphan_reaper, policy, pool,
protocol_recovery, recorder_lexical_ingest, recorder_migration, recorder_storage,
recording, replay, reports, restore_layout, restore_process, restore_scrollback,
retry, secrets, session_correlation, sharding, simulation, snapshot_engine,
spsc_ring_buffer, storage, survival, tailer, tantivy_ingest, tantivy_reindex,
telemetry, undo, vendored/mux_client, vendored/mux_pool, wait, watchdog,
webhook, wezterm, workflows/mod.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
